content: The patient has a red patchy, scaly rash around their mouth and nose that flares up periodically.
The patient's biotin level was found to be 492.5 Picograms per milliliter, in the bottom 10% of the normal range.
Plasma or serum biotin levels are not a reliable indicator of biotin status due to their insensitivity.
The gold standard test for biotin deficiency is beta-hydroxyisovalerate in the urine after a leucine challenge.
A urinary organic acid test with beta-hydroxyisovalerate on the list of analytes is recommended for a more accurate assessment of biotin status.
